items.situation: two friends debating whether a new cafe is worth the price

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Have you tried that new cafe on Elm Street? I went yesterday, and a small latte was six dollars.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Six dollars? That's almost double what the campus coffee shop charges. Was it actually any better?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Honestly, the coffee tasted about the same. But they have huge couches and fast Wi-Fi, so I studied there for three hours.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hmm. So you're kind of paying for the workspace, not the drink.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Exactly. For a quick coffee, it's not worth it. But on a day I need to focus, I'd happily go back.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: That makes sense. I've got a big paper due Friday, so maybe I'll give it a shot this week.

questions.stem: How much did the woman pay for a small latte?

options.C: Double on the campus shop's drinks
options.D: Nothing, the first one was free

questions.explanation: 女士明确说一杯小拿铁要六美元，所以A正确。B把校园咖啡店的较低价格误当成新咖啡馆的价格，属于wrong_detail。C混淆了她说的“几乎是校园店两倍”这一比较，而不是具体金额，属于misattributed。D凭空捏造了免费第一杯，对话中从未提及，属于related_but_not_stated。

questions.stem: Why does the man say he might visit the cafe this week?

options.A: He thinks the latte tastes much better there
options.B: He wants to buy one of the large couches
options.C: He needs a good place to work on a paper
options.D: He plans to meet the woman there tomorrow

questions.explanation: 男士提到周五有一篇大论文要交，正好对应女士说的“需要专注时值得去”，可推断他想要一个适合工作的环境，所以C正确。A与对话矛盾，女士说咖啡味道差不多，属于over_inference。B把“坐在大沙发上学习”误解成要购买沙发，属于over_inference。D凭空添加了见面安排，对话没有任何依据，属于unrelated_next_step。

items.situation: two friends discussing a movie one just saw with good twists but too long

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I finally saw that detective movie last night. The plot twists were amazing. I never guessed who the thief really was.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh, everyone's been talking about that ending. Was it as good as people say?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: The story, yes. But it ran almost three hours. By the last half hour, my legs were totally cramped and I just wanted it to end.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Three hours? That's really long for a mystery.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Right. If they'd cut twenty minutes, it would've been perfect. Still, I'd recommend it.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Okay. I'll wait for it to come out for streaming, then. That way I can pause whenever I need a break.

questions.stem: What did the woman dislike about the movie?

options.A: The plot twists were too confusing

questions.explanation: 女士说电影将近三小时，最后半小时她腿都麻了只想结束，所以B正确。A与对话矛盾，她称赞反转“amazing”，属于wrong_detail。C同样矛盾，她说结局很受好评且故事很好，属于wrong_detail。D凭空提到票价，对话从未涉及，属于related_but_not_stated。

questions.stem: Why does the man plan to wait for the streaming release?

options.A: He thinks the movie sounds boring
options.B: He cannot afford a movie ticket
options.C: He already saw it in the theater
options.D: He wants to be able to pause for breaks

questions.explanation: 男士说等流媒体上线就能随时暂停休息，直接回应电影太长这一问题，所以D正确。A与对话矛盾，他对反转和结局很感兴趣，属于over_inference。B凭空捏造经济原因，对话没有依据，属于unrelated_next_step。C与事实相反，他还没看过这部电影，属于rejected_option。

items.situation: two students comparing how they get to campus — bike, bus, or walk

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: How do you get to campus every morning? I've been taking the number nine bus, but it's always packed.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I used to ride the bus too, but now I bike. It takes about fifteen minutes, and I skip all the traffic.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Fifteen minutes? That's faster than my bus. But what about rainy days?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: On rainy days I just walk. It's only thirty minutes, and I bring an umbrella. Honestly, walking clears my head before class.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hmm, maybe I should buy a bike. The crowded bus really isn't worth it.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Good call. The shop near the library sells used ones cheap. You could check it out this weekend.

questions.stem: What are the two students mainly discussing?

options.A: How to repair a broken bicycle
options.B: Which bus route is the fastest
options.C: The different ways they travel to campus
options.D: Where to buy umbrellas near campus

questions.explanation: 整段对话围绕骑车、坐公交、步行等不同上学方式展开，所以C正确。A把结尾提到买车一带而过的内容夸大成主题，且话题是购买不是修理，属于wrong_topic。B只抓住公交这一个细节，忽略了整体比较，属于too_narrow。D把雨天带伞的次要细节当成主题，属于too_narrow。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do this weekend?

options.A: Visit the shop near the library to look at bikes
options.B: Buy a monthly pass for the number nine bus
options.C: Ask the man to give her a ride to campus
options.D: Sign up for a campus bus discount program

questions.explanation: 女士说也许该买辆自行车，男士建议她周末去图书馆旁的二手车店看看，可推断她会去那家店，所以A正确。B与她“拥挤公交不值”的态度相反，属于rejected_option。C凭空添加搭车安排，对话没有依据，属于unrelated_next_step。D捏造了一个公交折扣项目，对话从未提及，属于unrelated_next_step。

items.situation: two friends coordinating a weekend beach trip

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Are we still on for the beach on Saturday? I checked the weather, and it's supposed to be sunny all day.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Definitely. Should we leave early to beat the crowds?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Yeah, let's meet at the train station at eight. The ride is about an hour, so we'll get there by nine.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Sounds great. I'll bring the cooler and some sandwiches. Can you grab sunscreen and a couple of towels?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Sure, I've got those. Oh, and I'll borrow my brother's beach umbrella.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Perfect. Then we're all set. I'll text you Friday night just to confirm the time.

questions.stem: What will the man bring to the beach?
questions.explanation: 男士说他会带冷藏箱和三明治，所以D正确。A是女士负责带的东西，属于misattributed。B是女士说要向哥哥借的太阳伞，也属于misattributed。C凭空提到车票，对话从未说由谁购买，属于related_but_not_stated。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do on Friday night?

options.A: Buy the train tickets in advance
options.B: Send the woman a message to confirm the time
options.C: Pack the cooler and make sandwiches

questions.explanation: 男士明确说周五晚上会给女士发短信确认时间，所以B正确。A凭空添加买票动作，对话没有提到，属于unrelated_next_step。C是出发当天才做的准备，时间点不符，属于misattributed。D是女士已经做过的事，并非男士周五晚的计划，属于misattributed。

items.situation: two students talking about a houseplant one is caring for

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: My roommate left me in charge of her fern while she's home for break, and the leaves are turning brown at the tips. I'm kind of panicking.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Brown tips usually mean it's getting too much direct sun, or the air's too dry. Where do you keep it?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Right on the windowsill, in full afternoon sun. I figured plants love light.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Ferns actually prefer shade. Try moving it away from the window, and mist the leaves every couple of days.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Mist them? With plain water?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, just a spray bottle. It keeps the humidity up. Do that, and it should bounce back before she's even home.

questions.stem: What is the woman's main problem?

options.A: She doesn't know how to keep her roommate's fern healthy
options.B: She forgot to water the fern for several days
options.C: She wants to buy a new plant for the dorm
options.D: She is unhappy that her roommate went home

questions.explanation: 女士负责照看室友的蕨类植物，叶尖发黄发褐让她很慌，整段都在求助如何养好它，所以A正确。B捏造了忘记浇水的原因，问题其实是阳光太强空气太干，属于wrong_detail。C凭空提到买新植物，对话从未涉及，属于wrong_topic。D误读情境，她担心的是植物而非室友离开，属于reversed_situation。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Leave the fern in the sunny window
options.B: Buy a special fertilizer for the fern
options.C: Move the fern and mist it with water
options.D: Ask her roommate to come back early

questions.explanation: 男士建议把植物移离窗户并每隔几天用清水喷雾，女士也接受了这个办法，可推断她会照做，所以C正确。A与建议相反，男士说蕨类喜阴，属于rejected_option。B凭空添加施肥，男士只提到喷水保湿，属于over_inference。D捏造让室友提前回来，男士还说植物会在她回来前恢复，属于unrelated_next_step。
